rows.description: Computus Math. Hole Rectory a pondo Die
rows.notes: Top title of the page. Handwritten script is legible as a page heading; some letters uncertain (e.g. 'pondo') but clearly a header and contains no amounts.
Date line immediately under title; letters and numbers are clear. No monetary amounts present.
Section heading (appears to be 'Arreagies' / 'Arrearages'). No amounts. Word is slightly abbreviated/old spelling but clearly a header for the list below.
Right column clearly reads '60 0 0' aligned with this line. Letters of the description are legible enough to indicate a parish/item and 'pro Anno'. Alignment of vertical ruling lines unambiguous.
Amounts clearly read as '54 5 11'. Minor uncertainty in spelling of place name ('Longwithram' vs 'Longwitham'), but numeric columns and column alignment are clear.
Amount reads '18 0 0' clearly. Handwriting of description slightly stylized but reads as a parish name plus 'pro Anno'.
Amount '58 0 0' is clear and aligned. Place name spelling is approximate from script but is a header/entry line with a definite amount.
Amount '20 0 0' clear. Surname/abbreviation after 'Eddington' is smudged (I read 'R' or abbreviation), so description spelling uncertain but monetary columns are unambiguous.
Description line present ('Yarrington' or similar) but no amounts written in the pounds/shillings/pence columns for this row. Slight uncertainty in parish spelling due to cursive form.
Numeric columns read as '11 8 8'. Description likely 'Morton' or 'Merton' (handwriting ambiguous). Alignment to amount columns is consistent but the place-name letters are less distinct.
Amount '6 0 7' is clear in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. 'Chace Hill pro anno' reading of description is legible in the script.
Short description present (looks like 'Phrup' or 'Phupp'); no amounts visible. The word is faint and ambiguous; I classify as an entry because it is in the list under the section heading.
Amounts '5 7 2' are readable and aligned. Description appears as 'Pine quite South' (handwritten) — letters clear enough to capture approximate spelling.
Description 'Warner' is legible but no amount is present in the numeric columns for this row.

Amount '0 2 6' is clear. The name 'Ralph Marshalls Capong' is somewhat long-hand and cramped but readable enough to capture approximate spelling; some letters (e.g. 'Capong') uncertain but identifiable as a name.

Description reads as abbreviation 'Ld' (likely 'Ld' or 'Lo.'), amount columns read '3 15 9' though the pence digit has slight ink blotting; chosen reading fits column alignment and is valid (pence=9).
Line present with the description (possibly 'Calet' or 'Calet'), but no numeric amount visible. Description is faint and somewhat ambiguous.
Description (reads like 'Cuddington Mills') present; no amounts visible. Handwriting of 'Mills' and following word faint; classified as an entry.
The line 'Bampton pro Anno' is visible but there are no amounts in the right-hand columns for this row; script is somewhat faded.
Two placenames joined by '&' appear (reading approximated as 'Mountacuate & Lexanco'). No amounts visible and handwriting is faint/uncertain.
Amount reads '0 8 0' clearly. Description looks like 'Hustorp' or 'Hustor' — letters legible enough to record approximate spelling.
Amount '0 7 6' is clear. Description 'Greenway & Crimes' (or similar) is legible; '&' grouping with second name is visible.
Amount '0 10 0' clear. The place/name is faint and ambiguous; I transcribed as 'Danfifer pro hic' to reflect the observed letters, but original spelling uncertain.
Amount '0 16 0' clear. Description reads as 'Franklin pro vic' (or 'pro vic.' ), letters are reasonably legible.
Amount '0 16 0' is clear. Description 'Lower Hall & Anno' (or similar) readable; alignment confirms this amount belongs to this line.
Short, faint description present but illegible; no numeric values in the columns. I mark as an entry because it sits among other parish lines, but the text could not be confidently read.
Description appears to be 'Fader pro vic' or similar; there is no amount written. Letters are faint and somewhat ambiguous.

Pounds and shillings read as '4 9'. Pence area shows '2' followed by a small loop/ob symbol consistent with a halfpenny; interpreted as 2½d (pence whole=2, fraction=0.5). Description spelling ('Mollineaux') is approximate from cursive.

Pounds and shillings read '3 17'. Pence appears as '0' with a faint loop/mark which may indicate a halfpenny (interpreted as 0½d). The description is congested and uncertain ('Pitchard Popper' is an approximate reading).
Amount columns read '0 12 2' clearly. Description 'Clifton ferry' readable in cursive; alignment with numbers is clear.
Amounts appear as '4 15 2'. The description is longer and partly cramped/abbreviated (reads like 'Bonington pro vice & pro ...'); some trailing words are too faint to transcribe confidently.
A line with multiple words/abbreviations is visible but the text is faded and uncertain; no amounts visible in the numeric columns for this row. I provide an approximate reading of the description.

rows.description: Fletchewin pro bienio - per in part
rows.notes: Amount '6 0 0' is clear and aligned. The description includes 'pro bienio' and 'per in part' (or similar); the ending words are readable enough to convey that this is a partially accounted line.

rows.description: Tetchwin pro bienio - per in part
rows.notes: Amount reads '6 13 4' clearly. The name/description is similar to previous 'pro bienio' and includes 'per in part'—handwriting of the place name is somewhat ambiguous but the numeric columns are clear.
Several names appear here (my reading 'Tadbean Grock Morton' is approximate). No amounts are present on this line or they are too faint to detect; text is moderately ambiguous.

Bottom-right summary/total line shows numerals beginning '264 13' clearly for pounds and shillings. There is an extra small digit ('2') after the pence field which is ambiguous (could be a minor mark, a pence of '2', or a fractional marking). I have recorded pounds=264, shillings=13, pence=0 and left fraction blank because the small trailing mark is unclear; overall the total is readable but the very-low-rightmost marking reduces confidence.
